concepts.title: Peer-to-Peer Media
concepts.summary: A feature that allows Zoom clients and supported desk phones to send media (audio/video) directly to each other rather than routing through Zoom's servers. This can improve call quality and reduce latency, but introduces design considerations around feature compatibility (e.g., recording, monitoring) and the need for client restarts after toggling.
key_points: Media flows directly between devices instead of through Zoom servers
Available for Zoom clients and supported desk phones
Requires Zoom Workplace app version 6.7.0 or later
Client and device restart required for setting changes to take effect
Feature rolling out in phases over several months

user_roles.title: Account Owner or Admin
user_roles.summary: Has the privilege to enable or disable Peer-to-Peer Media at multiple hierarchy levels (account, group, site, and individual phone user). Admins can also lock the setting to prevent lower-level changes, making this role responsible for both configuration and governance of the feature.
key_points: Can configure at account, group, site, and individual user levels
Can lock settings to prevent changes at lower hierarchy levels
Requires edit privileges for the relevant scope (account settings, groups, etc.)
Must have a Zoom Workplace license with Phone or standalone Zoom Phone calling plan

task_flows.title: Enable or Disable Peer-to-Peer Media at Account Level
task_flows.summary: Admins navigate to Account Settings > Zoom Phone > General and toggle the Peer to Peer Media setting. A verification dialog confirms the change, and an optional lock prevents lower-level overrides. Designers should ensure the toggle, dialog, and lock icon are clearly associated and the grayed-out state at lower levels communicates the lock relationship.
key_points: Navigate: Account Management > Account Settings > Zoom Phone tab
Toggle Peer to Peer Media under General section
Confirm via verification dialog (Enable/Disable)
Optional: click lock icon to prevent user-level changes

task_flows.title: Enable or Disable Peer-to-Peer Media at Group Level
task_flows.summary: Similar to account-level flow but accessed through User Management > Groups. When the option is grayed out, a note explains it is locked at the account level. This pattern reinforces the lock hierarchy for admins managing subsets of users.
key_points: Navigate: User Management > Groups > select group > Zoom Phone tab
Toggle Peer to Peer Media under General section
Option grayed out if locked at account level, with explanatory note
Optional: click lock icon to prevent user-level changes within group

task_flows.title: Enable or Disable Peer-to-Peer Media at Site Level
task_flows.summary: Site-level configuration accessed through Phone System Management > Company Info. Follows the same toggle-and-lock pattern, but the navigation path differs due to site management being under Phone System Management rather than Account/User Management.
key_points: Navigate: Phone System Management > Company Info > select site > Policy tab
Toggle Peer to Peer Media under General section
Confirm via verification dialog
Optional: lock to prevent user-level changes within site

task_flows.title: Enable or Disable Peer-to-Peer Media for Individual Phone User
task_flows.summary: Individual user configuration through Phone System Management > Users & Rooms. When grayed out, the note explicitly tells the admin the setting is locked at group or account level and advises contacting the Zoom admin, providing clear guidance on next steps.
key_points: Navigate: Phone System Management > Users & Rooms > Users tab > select user > Policy tab
Toggle Peer to Peer Media under General section
Option grayed out if locked at group or account level
Grayed-out note instructs admin to contact higher-level admin

constraints.title: Client Version and Feature Compatibility Restrictions
constraints.summary: Peer-to-Peer media requires Zoom Workplace app version 6.7.0 or later, and is incompatible with certain features like recording or monitoring. Designers should consider how to communicate these incompatibilities — either by preventing toggling when incompatible features are active or by warning about the conflict.
key_points: Requires Zoom Workplace app version 6.7.0 or later
Incompatible with recording or monitoring features being enabled
Client and desk phone restart required after enabling/disabling
Feature rolling out in phases over the next few months

ux_patterns.title: Hierarchical Settings Lock
ux_patterns.summary: A cascading permission model where settings configured at a higher level (account) can be locked to prevent modification at lower levels (group, site, user). When locked, the toggle appears grayed out with an explanatory note. This pattern is used across Zoom Phone settings and should be consistently designed.
key_points: Lock cascades from account > group > site > user
Grayed-out toggle with descriptive note when locked at higher level
Lock icon displayed next to toggle when lockable
Note at user level explicitly directs admin to contact higher-level admin

ux_patterns.title: Verification Dialog for Toggle Changes
ux_patterns.summary: When an admin toggles a setting on or off, a verification dialog appears requiring explicit confirmation (Enable or Disable button). This prevents accidental changes and provides a confirmation step, which is especially important for settings that require device restarts.
key_points: Dialog appears after toggling a setting
Requires explicit Enable or Disable confirmation
Provides a safeguard against accidental configuration changes

ux_patterns.title: Multi-Level Settings Navigation
ux_patterns.summary: The same setting (Peer to Peer Media) is accessible through four different navigation paths depending on scope: Account Settings, Groups, Company Info (Sites), and Users & Rooms. Designers should ensure consistent toggle behavior and labeling across all these paths while allowing for context-appropriate navigation.
key_points: Account: Account Management > Account Settings > Zoom Phone > General
Group: User Management > Groups > [group] > Zoom Phone > General
Site: Phone System Management > Company Info > [site] > Policy > General
User: Phone System Management > Users & Rooms > Users > [user] > Policy > General
Toggle label, behavior, and verification dialog are consistent across all paths
